title: Hakeem's Hiccups
description: It’s Ramzan and Hakeem sahib has a case of the hiccups — so he can’t drink water to make it go away. What will hic-hic-hiccuping Hakeem sahib do, and can his patients help their beloved doctor?

pages.text:
In the bylanes of Sabzi Bazaar,
Amidst the rows of drying achaar,
Mixing powders and potions in pots of clay,
Hakeem sahib was busy seeing patients all day.

By evening the Hakeem’s lips were chapped.
His tummy was rumbling like a thunder clap.
A glass of water would have been divine.
But he was fasting, and it wasn’t yet time.

Listening to Sodawala’s tick-tocking heart,
Hakeem found his pulse was two beats apart.

As Hakeem sahib reached for his white pepper antidote,
He felt a bubble race all the way up his throat.

Hic
hic
hic!
Oh my, oh dear.
I’ll hold my breath,
I’ll pull my ear.
His hiccups came and went,
This was a most unusual event.
Hic
hic
hic!

Examining Machhiwali’s pale yellow eye,
Hakeem sahib wrote out a nuska for rye.
“Hold your breath,
pull your ear.”
Oh my,
oh dear.
Hic
hic
hic!

Mithaiwala showed up with a wart on his chest.
The Hakeem said a haldi poultice would work best.
“Hold your breath, pull your ear.”
Hic
hic
hic!
Oh my,
oh dear.

Snap. Crackle. Pop! Keep calm.
Hakeem sahib was setting the bone in Sabziwala’s arm.
“Hold your breath,
pull your ear.”
Oh my,
oh dear.
Hic
hic
hic

Little Munna came crying
with a tummy ache,
“Ajwain and saunf for you,
and no more cake!”
“Hold your breath, pull your ear.”
Oh my,
oh dear.
Hic
hic
hic!

As the hic-hic-hiccuping Hakeem locked up for the day,
He was surprised to see…

his patients in the way!
With colourful platters of food, each swore
To have a foolproof remedy of yore.
Machhiwali had cooked
a fiery red stew,
With spicy fish roasted
on the barbeque.
Sodawala had made a special lemonade,
Brimming with fizz and pomegranate.

Mithaiwala had brought a basket of roat,
Crunchy and sweet to coat the throat.
Sabziwala got karela
by the kilogram,
Something bitter to push
down the diaphragm.

As they argued as to which cure was best,
Out jumped a ghost, stirring unrest!
Machhiwali screamed, Sodawala jumped in fear,
Hic-hic, said the Hakeem loud and clear.
Hakeem sahib pulled off the white sheet,
And Munna beat a hasty retreat.
“Why? Oh, why this silly horseplay?”
“Amma says a good fright will scare the hiccups away!”

But wait, where did Hakeem sahib disappear?
Wasn’t he just… umm… right here?
Everyone peeked inside an open door,
Where Hakeem sahib’s family sat
cross-legged upon the floor.

Hand on his heart, he said, “Thank you, friends, for your kindness.
I would now like you all to join us.”
And, as if on cue, they all knew what to do.
They put down their platters and sat cross-legged, too.

Begum, Faria and Raheem brought cups and plates,
And the family handed everyone some dates.
As the azaan rang out from the east,
They dived into the waiting feast.

No more hic-hic-hiccuping Hakeem!
But wait, why does he look like he ate some neem?
“Oh no! I think a little bubble is stuck
and now it’s quickly making its way up!”

They all waited with bated breath,
As Hakeem sahib let out
a… Great... Big…
